subject: Language Arts
description: Spell words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child can spell words by sounding them out using letter sounds they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can say and make longer complete sentences during group language activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child can use common words like to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, and with.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use interrogatives to ask questions in full sentences (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child can ask questions using words like who, what, where, when, why, and how in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding 's' or 'es' sound.
description_plain: Your child can say plural nouns by adding 's' or 'es' sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use basic grammar rules when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Digital Communications begin in grade 3.
description_plain: Your child will start learning digital communication skills in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events with support.
description_plain: Your child can describe familiar people, places, things, and events with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, ask and answer questions to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions with help to get information or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally by asking and answering questions.
description_plain: Your child can check understanding by asking and answering questions about stories or information read aloud with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts with peers by following agreed upon rules for discussions; listening to others and taking turns speaking through at least two exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks about grade-level topics by listening, taking turns, and following simple rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Keyboarding skills begin in grade 3.
description_plain: Your child will start learning keyboarding skills in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet. Write left to right and top to bottom with appropriate spaces between letters.
description_plain: Your child can print all uppercase and lowercase letters, writing left to right and top to bottom with spaces between letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely write or dictate writing for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can regularly write or tell stories and information for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Listen to a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world.
description_plain: Your child listens to several related stories or texts to learn more about the world.

subject: Language Arts
description: Inquiry process begins in grade 1.
description_plain: Your child will start learning how to ask questions and explore topics in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading, and listening to texts.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can notice how words connect to real life, like spotting cozy places at home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words, like walk, march, or prance.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their synonyms and antonyms.
description_plain: Your child can understand common action and describing words by finding words that mean the same or opposite.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., foods, size) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can group everyday objects into categories, like foods or sizes, to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can explore how words relate to each other and their different meanings with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., discovering the verb "roll" is also a noun).
description_plain: Your child can find new meanings for familiar words and use them correctly, like knowing 'roll' can be a noun or a verb.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of new or tricky words in kindergarten-level reading and lessons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts heard on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find simple similarities and differences between two stories or texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons authors give to support points in texts heard.
description_plain: Your child can tell why authors give certain reasons to support their ideas in stories or texts they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of nonfiction texts.
description_plain: Your child can point out the front cover, back cover, and title page of nonfiction books.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in texts heard.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell key details of texts heard.
description_plain: Your child can retell important details from stories or texts they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, use evidence from nonfiction works read aloud to demonstrate of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use details from nonfiction books read aloud to show they understand the grade-level material with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures or experiences of characters in familiar stories heard.
description_plain: Your child can compare and talk about the adventures or experiences of characters in stories they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define the roles of authors and illustrators in presenting the ideas or information in stories.
description_plain: Your child can explain what authors and illustrators do to share ideas or information in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of stories.
description_plain: Your child can identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of storybooks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between characters, settings, and major events in stories heard.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters, places, and main events in stories are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell key details of familiar stories, poems, and nursery rhymes heard.
description_plain: Your child can retell important parts of familiar stories, poems, and nursery rhymes they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, use evidence from literature read aloud to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use details from stories read aloud to show they understand the grade-level material with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent-reader texts (e.g., rhymes, songs, simple poems) with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read simple texts like rhymes, songs, and poems with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in texts heard.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in stories or texts they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in listening to a series of texts related to the topics and themes being studied to build knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child regularly listens to several texts about the same topics to build knowledge and learn new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Text Complexity begins in grade 2.
description_plain: Your child will start reading more complex texts beginning in grade 2.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled CVC words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar short words by hearing the different letter sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common high-frequency words with automaticity by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).
description_plain: Your child can quickly recognize common sight words like 'the,' 'of,' and 'you' without needing to sound them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds for the five major vowel letters.
description_plain: Your child can connect the long and short sounds of the five main vowels (a, e, i, o, u).

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the most frequent sound for each consonant letter.
description_plain: Your child can say the most common sound for each consonant letter by matching one letter to one sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add, substitute, and delete individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change simple words by adding, changing, or taking away sounds to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) words and say the resulting word (Note: This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle, and last sounds in simple three-letter words and say the whole word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can blend and break apart the beginning and ending sounds of one-syllable spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, delete, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, say, blend, take away, and break apart syllables in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can find and say words that rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds.
description_plain: Your child understands how spoken words, syllables, and sounds work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can name all the uppercase and lowercase letters of the alphabet.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child knows what makes a sentence, like starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child understands that words in print are separated by spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can read print from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right with return sweep at the end of each line.
description_plain: Your child can follow words on a page from left to right and move to the next line when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know that print (not pictures) is what we read, and text holds meaning.
description_plain: Your child understands that we read the printed words, not the pictures, and that the words have meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child knows that spoken words are shown in writing by specific letters in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Locate a printed word on a page.
description_plain: Your child can find a printed word on a page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands the basic parts of printed text, like letters, words, and spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can spell new words by sounding them out and using spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common, taught spelling patterns and frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child spells common words correctly using patterns and remembers some irregular words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of spelling in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence, the first letter of student's name, and the pronoun "I".
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the first word in a sentence, their name, and the word 'I'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in dates and when listing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among declarative, exclamatory, and interrogative sentences, and use periods, exclamation marks, or question marks at the end of sentences when writing and reading text aloud.
description_plain: Your child knows different sentence types and uses the right punctuation at the end when writing or reading aloud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to create meaning.
description_plain: Your child uses punctuation and capitalization correctly to make writing clear when reading and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can write and make longer complete sentences when given a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, during, under, in, with, at).
description_plain: Your child uses common words like 'to', 'in', and 'with' to show relationships in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and', 'but', and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child uses common describing words (adjectives) in their speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their, anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'I', 'my', 'they', and 'anyone' to talk about people and things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple verb tenses (past, present, and future) for regular verbs.
description_plain: Your child uses simple past, present, and future verb forms correctly with regular verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Match single and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences. (e.g., He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child matches singular and plural nouns with the correct verbs in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use subject-verb agreement in simple sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses the right verb form to agree with the subject in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions English grammar and usage when writing and/or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses basic grammar rules correctly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Digital communications begins in grade 3
description_plain: Your child will start learning about digital communication in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child describes people, places, things, and events with clear details and shares feelings and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand better when listening to someone speak.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts with peers by listening to others closely, taking turns speaking through multiple exchanges, and asking questions to clear up any confusion.
description_plain: Your child takes part in group talks by listening carefully, taking turns, and asking questions to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Keyboarding skills begin in grade 3.
description_plain: Your child will start learning keyboarding skills in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print legibly and space words appropriately when writing a complete sentence.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly and spaces words correctly when writing full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Routinely write or dictate writing for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences (e.g., expressing a view or preference, supplying some information about the topic, stories that recount an event or tell a story).
description_plain: Your child regularly writes or tells stories and information for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read or listen to a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world.
description_plain: Your child reads or listens to several related texts to learn more about the world.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, conduct simple research tasks to take some action or make informal presentations by identifying information from classroom experiences or provided sources (including read alouds) and organizing information, recorded in words or pictures, using graphic organizers or other aids.
description_plain: With help, your child finds information from stories or classroom activities and organizes it to share or act on.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support as needed, acquire and use general academic and content-specific words gained through conversations, reading, and listening to texts.
description_plain: With some help, your child learns and uses new academic and subject words from talking, reading, and listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or describe senses like sight and sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, stroll, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands different action words that mean similar things by acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring grade-level verbs and adjectives by relating them to their synonyms and antonyms.
description_plain: Your child understands common verbs and adjectives by learning their similar and opposite words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (e.g., tools, pets) and define those words by one or more key attributes (e.g., a saw is a tool that cuts; a goldfish is a pet that lives in water).
description_plain: Your child groups words by categories like tools or pets and explains what makes them special.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child explores how words are related and the different meanings they can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., playpen, penpal).
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about words to guess the meaning of compound words like 'playpen' or 'penpal'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently encountered root words (e.g., help) and use the roots as clues to the meaning of the full word (e.g., helper, helpful).
description_plain: Your child recognizes common root words and uses them to understand related words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read frequently encountered words with inflectional endings (e.g., -d, -ed, -s, -es).
description_plain: Your child can read words with endings like -ed, -s, or -es that change the word form.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes (e.g., re-, un- pre-, -ful, -less) as clues to the nuance they add to known words.
description_plain: Your child can use common beginnings and endings of words (like re-, un-, -ful, -less) to understand what the words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the rest of the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to help understand the meaning of words and phrases in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies:
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts heard or read on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find simple similarities and differences between two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons authors give to support points in texts heard or read.
description_plain: Your child can identify reasons authors give to support their ideas in texts they hear or read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., table of contents, headings, glossaries, icons, index) to locate information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book like the table of contents, headings, glossary, and index to find information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in texts heard or read.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or pieces of information are connected in what they read or hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell key details of texts that demonstrate understanding of the main topics of texts heard or read.
description_plain: Your child can retell important details from texts to show they understand the main topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from nonfiction works to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use facts from nonfiction books to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures or experiences of characters in stories heard.
description_plain: Your child can compare and talk about the adventures or experiences of characters in stories they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe who is telling stories heard or read at various points in texts.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different parts of a book or story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between books that tell stories and books that give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between characters, settings, and major events in stories heard, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters, places, and important events are connected in stories they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell the beginning, middle, and end of familiar stories (including fables and fairy tales) with key details heard or read, demonstrating understanding of their central messages or morals.
description_plain: Your child can retell the beginning, middle, and end of familiar stories and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from literature to demonstrate. understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use details from stories to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression to support comprehension in successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books accurately and with expression to help understand the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in texts heard or read.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in stories or texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading and listening to a series of texts related to the topics and themes being studied to build knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child will regularly read and listen to books about the same topics to learn new words and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Text complexity begins in grade 2.
description_plain: Your child will start reading more challenging books in grade 2.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words (e.g., what, said, have).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules, like 'what' and 'said.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode frequently encountered words with inflectional endings (e.g., -s, ed, -est).
description_plain: Your child can read words with endings like -s, -ed, and -est.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words by breaking them into parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Learn all the r-controlled vowel patterns (-ar, -er, -ir, -or, -ur) and recognize how they change short vowel recognition and pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child learns special vowel sounds like ar, er, ir, or, and ur and how they change word sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and can count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds (e.g., ai, ay, ee, ea, oa, and oe).
description_plain: Your child knows that some vowels work together or have a silent 'e' to make long vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can read simple one-syllable words that follow regular spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how common letter pairs like 'sh' and 'ch' sound when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment and blend sequences of individual sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delete initial and final sounds in spoken single syllable words and say the resulting word.
description_plain: Your child can say a word after taking away the first or last sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and say the first, middle, and last sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds, including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say one-syllable words by blending sounds together, including blends like 'bl' or 'st.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made of sounds and syllables when listening and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources to check and fix spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned when writing new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of spelling in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about spelling to help write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, names, and places.
description_plain: Your child knows to capitalize holidays, names, and places when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apostrophes to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes to write contractions (like can't) and show possession (like the dog's bone).

subject: Language Arts
description: Commas in greetings and closing of letters.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly in the greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to create meaning.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use punctuation and capitalization correctly when writing and reading aloud to make their meaning clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write and expand simple and compound sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between complete and incomplete sentences and recognize and use correct word order in written sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between complete and incomplete sentences and use the right word order when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., yourself, herself).
description_plain: Your child can use reflexive pronouns like yourself and herself correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that the names of things can also be the names of actions (fish, dream, run).
description_plain: Your child understands that some words can be names of things or actions, like fish, dream, and run.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., men, teeth, fish).
description_plain: Your child can form and use regular and common irregular plural nouns like men, teeth, and fish.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use adjectives and adverbs and choose the right one to describe something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., felt, told, went).
description_plain: Your child can form and use the past tense of common irregular verbs like felt, told, and went.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct English grammar when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Digital Communications begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Digital communication skills start in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or retell an experience with relevant facts and descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience with important details, speaking clearly in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says to clarify by gathering additional information or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to understand better or get more information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe main ideas and details from stories or information they hear or watch.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts with peers by gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others closely and building on others' ideas, and asking for clarification and further explanation to ensure understanding.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks about grade-level topics by taking turns, listening carefully, building on others' ideas, and asking questions to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, use keyboarding skills to produce and publish writing.
description_plain: With help, your child can use keyboarding skills to write and share their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form letters correctly with functional speed. Space words and sentences properly so that writing can be read easily by another person.
description_plain: Your child can write letters correctly and space words and sentences so others can read their writing easily.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support from adults and peers, strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can improve their writing by revising and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Employing clear and coherent organizational structures begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Organizing writing clearly starts in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional stories that recount a short sequence of events, include details to develop the characters or experiences, and provide sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write personal or made-up stories that tell a short series of events, include details about characters or experiences, and have a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informational texts that state a focus and support the focus with facts and details and provide a concluding sentence.
description_plain: Your child can write informational texts that focus on a topic, support it with facts and details, and end with a concluding sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that express an opinion supported by details and reasons and provide a concluding sentence.
description_plain: Your child can write opinions supported by reasons and details, and finish with a concluding sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely engaging in the production of writing shorter and longer pieces for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences. This could include reflections, descriptions, letters, and poetry, etc.
description_plain: Your child practices writing different lengths and types of pieces like reflections, descriptions, letters, and poems for various purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read or listen to a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world. (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.)
description_plain: Your child reads or listens to groups of related texts to learn about the world, with some texts easy enough to read alone and others with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support as needed, conduct short research tasks to take some action or make informal presentations by gathering information from experiences and provided sources (including read alouds), and organizing information using graphic organizers or other aids.
description_plain: With some help, your child can do short research projects by gathering information from experiences and sources, and organizing it to share.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use general academic and content-specific words gained through conversations, and reading and listening to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe situations with specificity (e.g., When other kids are acting silly, that makes me feel giddy). Use these words in discussions and writing.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words from talking, reading, and listening, including describing situations with adjectives and adverbs, and uses these words when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases (e.g., rhymes, alliteration) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how rhymes and repeated sounds add rhythm and meaning to stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., hot, sizzling, blazing).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar verbs like toss, throw, and hurl, and similar adjectives like hot, sizzling, and blazing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe weather that is freezing or windy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like describing weather as freezing or windy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words and phrases provide meaning and nuance to texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how words and phrases add meaning and subtle differences to texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, print or digital, to clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use glossaries and simple dictionaries, in print or online, to find out what words and phrases mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., backpack, backyard; flashlight, lighthouse).
description_plain: Your child can guess the meaning of compound words like backpack or lighthouse by knowing the meanings of the smaller words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., pain/painful, help/helpless).
description_plain: Your child can use the meaning of a root word to help understand a new word with the same root, like pain and painful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of new words formed when known prefixes (e.g., safe/unsafe, like/dislike) and suffixes (e.g., beauty/beautiful, light/lightness) are added to a known word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by adding prefixes or suffixes to words they know, like safe/unsafe or beauty/beautiful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as clues to the meaning of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to understand the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies based on what they read or learn.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell the differences and similarities between the main points in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how authors use facts and reasons to support specific points in texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors use facts and reasons to support their main points in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of nonfiction texts heard or read, including identifying how the beginning introduces information and the ending sums up the information.
description_plain: Your child can describe how nonfiction texts are organized, including how the beginning introduces information and the ending sums it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific concepts, or steps in technical procedures in texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a series of events, scientific ideas, or steps in instructions are connected in texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central idea of texts heard or read.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea in stories or texts they hear or read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from nonfiction works to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child uses facts from nonfiction books to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (heard or read) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can tell how two or more versions of the same story are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify different perspectives of characters in stories heard or read.
description_plain: Your child understands how different characters in a story see things in their own way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of stories heard or read, including identifying how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a story starts, what happens in the middle, and how it ends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in stories heard or read respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child describes how characters react to big events or problems in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the central message, lesson, or moral of stories (including fables and folktales) from diverse cultures heard or read.
description_plain: Your child can find the lesson or moral in stories from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from literature to demonstrate understanding grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child uses details from stories to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression to support comprehension in successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books clearly and with expression to help understand the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in grade-level texts heard or read.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how about stories they hear or read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in reading and listening to a series of texts, independently, with peers, or with modest support related to the topics and themes being studied to build knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child regularly reads or listens to books on topics they are learning to build knowledge and new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend texts representing a balance of genres, cultures, and perspectives, that exhibit complexity at the lower end of the grades 2–3 band.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands different kinds of books on their own, including stories from various cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words (e.g., was, again, been), including silent letter combinations.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads tricky words that don’t follow usual spelling rules, like 'was' or 'been'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child notices words that have unusual but common ways to match letters and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes (e.g., un-, dis-, -ful, -less).
description_plain: Your child reads words with common beginnings and endings like 'un-' or '-ful'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long and short vowels.
description_plain: Your child reads two-syllable words with long and short vowel sounds correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for common short and long vowel teams (e.g., head, hook, boat, weigh) including diphthongs (e.g., toil, cloud).
description_plain: Your child knows how to say and spell common vowel sounds and vowel teams in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about sounds and letters to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate automaticity in the deletion and substitution of phonemes in multi-syllable spoken words and naming of resulting words.
description_plain: Your child can quickly change or take away sounds in spoken multi-syllable words and say the new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Reverse phonemes in spoken one-syllable words (e.g., reverse initial and final consonants in the word "pat" and say the resulting word).
description_plain: Your child can switch sounds around in one-syllable words and say the new word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made of sounds and syllables when listening and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials to check and correct spelling.
description_plain: Your child uses books or tools to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell high-frequency irregular words correctly (e.g., who, what, why).
description_plain: Your child spells common tricky words like 'who' and 'why' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) when pronouncing and writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling rules and patterns to say and write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words.
description_plain: Your child spells common words and adds endings like suffixes the right way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of spelling in writing.
description_plain: Your child uses their spelling knowledge when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use capital letters in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Forming and using possessives.
description_plain: Your child can write and use possessive words to show ownership.

subject: Language Arts
description: Commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child uses commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Commas in addresses and dates.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in addresses and dates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to create meaning.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation and capitalization to make writing clear and easy to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in full sentences to give details or explain when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange simple and compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer or change their order to improve writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and', 'but', and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses different kinds of nouns, including names and words that show ownership.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns (e.g., fish, teeth).
description_plain: Your child knows how to make nouns plural, including irregular ones like 'fish' and 'teeth'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., family, crew, assembly) matched to plural verb forms.
description_plain: Your child uses group nouns like 'family' or 'crew' with the right verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs.
description_plain: Your child uses words that compare things, like 'bigger' or 'fastest'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive and perfect verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child uses verb tenses that show ongoing or completed actions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained digitally to determine where, when, why, and how key events occur.
description_plain: Your child can use information found online to explain where, when, why, and how important events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support, evaluate whether a digital source is factual or not by considering its use of evidence.
description_plain: With help, your child can decide if information from a website or digital source is true by checking the evidence it gives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly and at a good speed to tell about a topic, share a story, or describe an experience with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says, adding details to show understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in a variety of media (audio, visual, and quantitative).
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas and important details from stories read aloud or from videos, pictures, and charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts with peers by staying on topic; linking comments to the remarks of others; asking questions to check understanding of information being discussed; and reviewing ideas expressed.
description_plain: Your child can join group talks about grade-level topics by staying on topic, connecting ideas, asking questions, and reviewing what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use keyboarding skills to produce and publish writing.
description_plain: Your child can use a keyboard to write and share their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly in cursive, leaving space between letters in a word, in a sentence, and at the edges of the paper.
description_plain: Your child can write in cursive clearly, leaving space between letters, words, sentences, and at the edges of the paper.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing. (Editing should demonstrate command of grade-level Grammar and Conventions.)
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can plan, change, and fix their writing to make it better, using correct grammar and punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Group related information within a paragraph, using common linking words and phrases to connect ideas and information.
description_plain: Your child can group related ideas in a paragraph and use words that connect those ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional stories that recount an event or experience, include details to develop the characters or event(s), and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write personal or made-up stories that tell about an event, include details about characters or what happened, and have a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informational texts that introduce the topic, develop the focus with facts and details, and provide a concluding statement.
description_plain: Your child can write informational texts that introduce a topic, give facts and details, and end with a closing statement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that introduce the topic, express an opinion supported with facts, details, and reasons, and provide a concluding statement.
description_plain: Your child can write opinions about a topic, support them with facts and reasons, and finish with a concluding statement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely engaging in the production of shorter and longer pieces for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences. This could include, among others, summaries, reflections, descriptions, letters, and poetry, etc.
description_plain: Your child can practice writing different kinds of pieces, both short and long, for various reasons and audiences, like summaries, letters, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world. (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.)
description_plain: Your child can read several texts about related topics to learn more about the world, choosing books they can read alone, with friends, or with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research tasks to take some action or share findings orally or in writing by gathering and recording information on a specific topic from reference texts or through interviews, and using text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects by gathering information from books, interviews, or online tools and then share what they find by speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use general academic and content-specific words and phrases occurring in grade-level reading and content, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., She stood behind the door before she entered the room). Use these words in discussions and writing.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use important academic and subject words, including words that show location and time, when talking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among grade-appropriate, related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar words that show feelings or how sure someone is, like 'knew' and 'believed.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).
description_plain: Your child can understand when words or phrases mean exactly what they say and when they mean something different, like 'take steps.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words and phrases provide meaning and nuance to grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how words and phrases add meaning and details to what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, print or digital, to clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use glossaries or beginner dictionaries, in books or online, to find the exact meaning of important words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., transport, portable).
description_plain: Your child can use a word they know to help understand a new word that has the same root, like 'transport' and 'portable.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of new words formed when known affixes are added to a known word (e.g., expensive/ inexpensive, lock/unlock, help/helpless, care/ careless).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new words by looking at prefixes or suffixes added to words they know, like 'help' and 'helpless.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as clues to the meaning of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use the words around an unknown word in a sentence to help understand what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade-level reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies while reading and learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast important points and key supporting details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell the differences and similarities between important points and details in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the logical connection between particular facts and reasons in texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how facts and reasons in a text are connected logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe major structural differences between the organization of different informational texts (e.g., description, sequence, comparison, problem-solution, cause-effect).
description_plain: Your child can describe how different informational texts are organized, like by description, order, comparison, problem and solution, or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of events, concepts, steps, or procedures in historical, scientific, or technical texts, using words that pertains to comparison, sequence, or cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events, ideas, or steps are related in history, science, or technical texts using words about order, comparison, or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe key details from texts and explain how they support the central idea.
description_plain: Your child can describe important details from texts and explain how they support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from nonfiction works to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use facts and details from nonfiction books to show they understand grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters.
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell the differences and similarities between themes, settings, and plots in stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the difference between a narrator's point of view and various characters' perspectives in stories.
description_plain: Your child can explain the difference between the narrator's point of view and the different characters' views in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major structural differences between poems, plays, and prose.
description_plain: Your child can explain how poems, plays, and stories are organized differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how characters develop (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) throughout the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how characters change or grow in a story by looking at their traits, feelings, or reasons for acting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe key details from stories (including folktales, fables, and tall tales) from diverse cultures and explain how they support the central lesson, moral, or theme.
description_plain: Your child can describe important details from stories like folktales and fables from different cultures and explain the main lesson or theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from literature to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use details from stories and literature to show they understand grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and expression in successive readings to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts accurately, smoothly, and with expression to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts, referring explicitly to textual evidence as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about grade-level texts using evidence from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in a volume of reading (independently, with peers, or with modest support) related to the topics and themes being studied to build knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can read a lot of books and texts about topics being studied, alone or with others, to build knowledge and learn new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend texts representing a balance of genres, cultures, and perspectives that exhibit complexity at the higher end of the grades 2–3 band.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different types of stories and texts from various cultures and viewpoints that are challenging for grades 2–3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words (e.g., come, friend, today).
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level words that don’t follow usual spelling rules, like 'come,' 'friend,' and 'today.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllable words.
description_plain: Decode multisyllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Greek and Latin roots (e.g., trans, port, bio).
description_plain: Your child can read words by recognizing common Greek and Latin parts, like 'trans,' 'port,' and 'bio.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words when known affixes are added to a known word (e.g., visit/revisit, appear/disappear, lead/mislead, care/careful).
description_plain: Your child can read words by breaking them down when prefixes or suffixes are added to words they know, like 'revisit' or 'careful.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of grade-level phonics and word analysis skills to decode words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts they’ve learned to figure out how to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade level words correctly, including commonly confused words (e.g., there/their/they're).
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words correctly, including words that sound alike but have different meanings like 'there,' 'their,' and 'they're.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.
description_plain: Use correct capitalization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use quotation marks to show when someone is speaking or when words are taken from a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Commas in a series.
description_plain: Commas in a series.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to create meaning.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading out loud to make their meaning clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child understands when to use formal English, like during presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like in small group chats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences; recognize and correct inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child writes complete sentences and can fix sentences that are incomplete or too long without proper punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb agreement.
description_plain: Your child makes sure the subject and verb in a sentence agree, like using 'he runs' instead of 'he run.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused common words (e.g., to/too/two).
description_plain: Your child uses commonly confused words correctly, such as 'to,' 'too,' and 'two.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can make and use prepositional phrases, like 'under the table' or 'next to the park.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns and relative adverbs.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'who,' 'which,' and 'where' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns.
description_plain: Your child puts adjectives in the right order when describing things, like 'a big red ball' instead of 'a red big ball.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use principal modals to convey various conditions (e.g., can, may, must).
description_plain: Your child uses helping words like 'can,' 'may,' and 'must' to show different meanings in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize subject-predicate relationship in sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands how sentences are made of a subject (who or what) and a predicate (what they do or are).

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, timelines, or interactive elements) on Web pages.
description_plain: Your child can understand information shown in pictures, charts, graphs, timelines, or interactive parts on websites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate whether a digital source is factual or opinion-based by considering its use of evidence and whose point-of-view is represented or missing.
description_plain: Your child can tell if information from the internet is a fact or an opinion by looking at the evidence and whose views are shared or missing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly and in order about a topic, story, or experience, using facts and details to support their main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points being made.
description_plain: Your child can identify the reasons and evidence a speaker uses to support their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud, or information presented in diverse media (audio, visual, and quantitative).
description_plain: Your child can restate parts of a text or information they hear or see in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts with peers by carrying out assigned roles; making comments that build on and link to others' remarks; clarifying or following up on information; and reviewing key ideas expressed and explaining one's understanding.
description_plain: Your child participates in group talks about grade-level topics by sharing ideas, asking questions, and explaining what they understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing, demonstrating sufficient command of keyboarding skills.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or tablets to write and publish their work, showing good keyboard skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly and fluently in cursive by hand, forming letters and words that can be easily read by others.
description_plain: Your child writes in cursive clearly and smoothly so others can easily read their handwriting.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing. (Editing should demonstrate command of grade-level Grammar and Conventions.)
description_plain: With help from adults or friends, your child plans, revises, and edits their writing to make it better, using correct grammar and punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Organize related information together in paragraphs using precise language and linking words and phrases to connect details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child organizes related information into paragraphs and uses clear words and connecting phrases to link ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional narratives that organize the writing around a central problem, conflict, or experience; use descriptions or dialogue to develop the characters or event(s); and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events that focus on a main problem or experience, using descriptions or dialogue and ending with a clear conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informational texts that introduce the topic; develop the focus with facts, details or other information; and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes informational texts that introduce a topic, give facts and details, and end with a summary or conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that introduce the topic; express a clear opinion supported with facts, details and reasons; and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes opinions about a topic, supports their ideas with facts and reasons, and finishes with a concluding statement.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely engaging in the production of shorter and longer pieces for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences. This could include, among others, summaries, reflections, descriptions, letters, and poetry, etc.
description_plain: Your child practices writing different kinds of pieces, both short and long, for various purposes and audiences, like summaries, letters, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world. (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.)
description_plain: Your child reads several texts about related topics to learn more about the world, choosing books they can read alone, with friends, or with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research tasks to take some action or share findings orally or in writing by identifying what information is needed to answer a research question, using text features and search tools to gather relevant information efficiently; and taking notes, categorizing that information, and providing a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects by figuring out what information is needed, using tools to find it, taking notes, organizing facts, and listing sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and content-specific words and phrases occurring in grade-level reading and content, including those that signal precise actions or states of being (e.g., frustrated, puzzled, stammered) and vocabulary essential to a particular topic (e.g., heroes, villains, quest, fate when discussing myths). Use these words in discussions and writing.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses important academic and topic-specific words in reading, talking, and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe subtle differences (e.g., shook, trembled, wavered, quivered).
description_plain: Your child understands and explains small differences in similar words, like 'shook,' 'trembled,' and 'quivered.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of idioms, adages, and proverbs in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains the meaning of common sayings, like idioms, adages, and proverbs, based on how they are used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words and phrases provide meaning and nuance to grade-level texts:
description_plain: Your child figures out how words and phrases add meaning and detail to texts they read at grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), print or digital, to find the pronunciation and clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say words and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of words (e.g., thermometer, thermos, thermostat).
