description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out the meanings of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as clues to the meaning of words or phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use clues like definitions or examples in a text to figure out what words or phrases mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies:
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies suited for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Combine information from two texts on the same topic, noting important similarities and differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can put together information from two texts about the same topic and notice important similarities and differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how authors use evidence and reasons to support specific points in texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors use facts and reasons to support their points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the overall structure of informational texts (e.g., description, sequence, comparison, problem-solution, cause-effect) and how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child understands how informational texts are organized and how each part builds on what came before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, steps, ideas, or concepts found in historical, scientific, or technical texts, including what happened and why.
description_plain: Your child can explain what happened and why in historical, scientific, or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas of texts and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize texts.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in texts, explain how details support them, and summarize what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from nonfiction works to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from nonfiction books to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and patterns of events in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare how similar themes and events are treated in stories and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can tell how different stories are told from different points of view, like first-person or third-person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the overall structures of stories, plays, and poems and how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child understands how stories, plays, and poems are organized and how each part connects to the ones before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe a character, setting, or event in depth in stories and plays, drawing on specific details in the texts (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, settings, or events in detail using specific examples from stories and plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central themes in stories (including myths and legends), poems, and plays and explain how they are supported by key details.
description_plain: Your child can find the main themes in stories, poems, and plays and explain how details support those themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from literature to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use examples from literature to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and expression in successive readings to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts smoothly, accurately, and with expression to help understand the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in grade-level texts when explaining what texts say explicitly and when drawing inferences from texts.
description_plain: Your child can point to details and examples in texts to explain what the text says and to make guesses about its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in a volume of reading, independently, with peers, or with modest support related to the topics and themes being studied to build knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child reads a lot on their own or with others about topics they are studying to build knowledge and learn new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend texts representing a balance of genres, cultures, and perspectives that exhibit complexity at the lower end of the grades 4–5 band.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different kinds of texts from various cultures and genres at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar grade-appropriate multisyllabic words (e.g., depart, beneficial, recycle) in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about letters, syllables, and word parts to read new, longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-level words correctly, including commonly confused words (e.g., its/it's, affect/effect).
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words correctly, including words that are often confused like its/it's or affect/effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child knows how to show titles of books, stories, or other works by underlining, using quotation marks, or italics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Commas to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence (e.g., Yes, thank you, It's true, isn't it?).
description_plain: Your child can use commas to separate introductory words or phrases from the rest of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Commas before a coordinating conjunction.
description_plain: Your child can use commas before words like and, but, or, when joining parts of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to create meaning.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation and capitalization correctly when writing and reading aloud to make their meaning clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, using formal language when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or more interesting to keep the reader or listener engaged.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating (e.g., and, but), subordinating (e.g., although, because), and correlative (e.g., either/or) conjunctions to join words and phrases in a sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use different joining words like and, but, although, because, either/or to connect words and phrases in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child makes sure subjects and verbs agree and pronouns match the nouns they replace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, adverbs, conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections appropriate to function.
description_plain: Your child uses different parts of speech like nouns, verbs, adjectives, and conjunctions correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense and number.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when verb tenses or numbers change incorrectly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use irregular verbs (e.g., lie/lay, sit/set, rise/raise) correctly in sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use irregular verbs like lie/lay or sit/set correctly when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or beauty of a text presented digitally.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures, videos, or other digital elements add meaning or feeling to a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information from multiple digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can find answers or solve problems by using information from different digital sources efficiently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consider the source of information gathered digitally through such means as domains (e.g., .gov; .edu vs. .com or .tv).
description_plain: Your child knows how to check where digital information comes from to decide if it is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an argument, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant descriptive details to support main ideas or themes and speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can give clear oral reports or arguments, organizing ideas logically and using facts and details to support them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the major points a speaker makes and explain how each is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can summarize the main points a speaker makes and explain how those points are supported by reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information from texts read aloud or presented in different ways, like videos or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts with peers by carrying out assigned roles; making comments and posing and responding to questions that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on others' remarks; and reviewing key ideas expressed and drawing conclusions considering the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group discussions by sharing ideas, asking and answering questions, and helping the group reach conclusions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing demonstrating sufficient command of keyboarding skills.
description_plain: Your child can use technology to write and publish their work, showing good keyboarding skills.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write in cursive legibly and fluently by hand with a consistent form and recognizable signature.
description_plain: Your child can write in cursive clearly and smoothly, with consistent letter shapes and a signature that can be recognized.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach. (Editing should demonstrate command of grade-level Grammar and Conventions.)
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or rewriting with help from adults or friends, using correct grammar and punctuation for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent organizational structures of multiple paragraphs in which facts and details are logically grouped and linking words and phrases connect details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can write several paragraphs that are organized clearly, grouping facts and details logically and using words that connect ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional narratives that establish a situation and narrator; organize around a central problem, conflict, or experience using descriptions, dialogue or pacing to develop the characters, event(s), or experience(s); and provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated events.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imaginary events that have a clear situation and narrator, focus on a main problem or experience, use descriptions and dialogue to develop characters and events, and end with a fitting conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informational texts that introduce the topic; develop the focus with relevant facts, details, and examples from multiple sources that are logically grouped, including headings to support the purpose; and provide a concluding section.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, include facts and examples from different sources grouped logically with headings, and finish with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that introduce the topic clearly; express a distinct opinion supported with adequate facts, ideas, and reasons that are logically grouped and provide a concluding section.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that clearly state their topic and opinion, support it with facts and reasons grouped logically, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely engaging in the production of shorter and longer pieces for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences. This could include, among others, summaries, reflections, descriptions, critiques, letters, and poetry, etc.
description_plain: Your child can write different kinds of short and long pieces, like summaries, reflections, descriptions, letters, or poems, for different tasks and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world. (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.)
description_plain: Your child can read several texts about related topics to learn more about the world, choosing books they can read alone, with friends, or with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research tasks to take some action or share findings orally or in writing by formulating research questions; gathering relevant and reliable information from both primary and secondary sources as appropriate; paraphrasing and quoting ideas and information; and respecting copyright guidelines for use of that information and any images.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects by asking questions, finding trustworthy information from different sources, using their own words or quotes, and following rules about using others' work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and content-specific words and phrases occurring in grade-level reading and content, including those that signal contrast, addition, connection, and other logical relationships (e.g., therefore, for example, meanwhile, on the other hand). Use these words in discussions and writing.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use important academic and subject-specific words, including words that show connections like 'therefore' or 'for example,' in talking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe different states or subtleties (e.g., sang, trilled, chirped, chorused).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar words that show different shades of meaning, like 'sang,' 'trilled,' or 'chirped.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of figurative language such as metaphors and similes, in context.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain the meaning of figurative language like metaphors and similes when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words and phrases provide meaning and nuance to grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how words and phrases add meaning and subtle differences to grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), print or digital, to find the pronunciation and clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say words and understand their exact meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of words (e.g., biography, biology, biohazard).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as clues to the meaning of words or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues in the text, like definitions or examples, to understand the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clear up the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words by using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same event or topic to demonstrate a coherent understanding of the information.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from several texts about the same topic to show they understand the information clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how authors use evidence and reasons to support specific claims in texts, identifying which reasons and evidence support which claims.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors use reasons and evidence to support their claims and identify which evidence supports which claim.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how series of chapters or sections fit together to provide the overall structure of informational texts (e.g., description, sequence, comparison, problem-solution, cause-effect).
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters or sections fit together to show the overall structure of informational texts, like cause and effect or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in historical, scientific, or technical texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two or more people, events, ideas, or concepts relate or interact in history, science, or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the central ideas of texts, including how they are developed using details; summarize texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main ideas of texts, how details develop those ideas, and summarize what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from nonfiction works to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from nonfiction books to show they understand grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre on their approaches to similar themes.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast stories in the same type by looking at how they handle similar themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influence how events are described in stories, plays, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a narrator's or speaker's point of view affects how events are described in stories, plays, or poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how chapters, scenes, or stanzas work together to provide the overall structure of a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or stanzas work together to create the overall structure of a literary text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in stories and plays, drawing on specific details in the texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in stories and plays using specific details from the texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a text and determine the central themes of stories, plays, or poems, including how they are developed using details.
description_plain: Your child can summarize a story, play, or poem and identify its main themes, explaining how details develop those themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from literature to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from literature to show they understand grade-level texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and expression in successive readings to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts accurately, smoothly, and with expression to help understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from grade-level texts to explain what is said explicitly and when drawing inferences, including quoting from texts accurately.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain information from grade-level texts, including quoting exact words and making inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in a volume of reading, independently, with peers, or with modest support related to the topics and themes being studied to build knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child regularly reads a lot, alone or with others, about topics and themes they are studying to build knowledge and vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend texts representing a balance of genres, cultures, and perspectives that exhibit complexity at the higher end of the grades 4–5 band.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand a variety of texts from different genres and cultures that are challenging for grades 4–5.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar grade-level multisyllabic words (e.g., disallow, misinform, transaction) in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use their knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read unfamiliar multisyllabic words correctly in and out of context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell derivatives correctly by applying knowledge of bases and affixes.
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly by using their knowledge of word bases and endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Colons to separate hours and minutes and to introduce a list.
description_plain: Your child can use colons correctly to separate hours and minutes and to introduce lists.

subject: Language Arts
description: Commas, parentheses, and dashes to set off nonrestrictive or parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, parentheses, and dashes to set off extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to create meaning.
description_plain: Your child can use correct punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to make their meaning clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child can notice when language is different from standard English in their own and others' speaking and writing and use strategies to improve it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, or reduce sentences (e.g., adding or deleting modifiers, combining, or breaking up sentences) for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can change sentences by adding, combining, or breaking them up to make their meaning clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when pronouns change in number or person in the middle of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns that don’t clearly show who or what they refer to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use pronouns correctly regarding case, number, and person, including intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns correctly, matching their case, number, and person, including words like myself or ourselves.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that a word performs different functions according to its position in the sentence.
description_plain: Your child understands that a word can have different jobs in a sentence depending on where it is placed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the eight basic parts of speech (noun, pronoun, verb, adverb, adjective, conjunction, preposition, interjection).
description_plain: Your child can name and identify the eight main parts of speech, like nouns, verbs, and adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include digital components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, music, or sounds to presentations to help explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story to a digital version, contrasting what is "seen" and "heard" when reading the text with what is perceived when listened to or watched.
description_plain: Your child compares a written story to a digital version by noticing differences in what they see, hear, or watch.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow safety practices and ethical guidelines when gathering, sharing, and using information.
description_plain: Your child follows safety rules and behaves responsibly when finding, sharing, and using information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consider the source of information gathered digitally through such means as domains (e.g., .gov; .edu vs. .com or .tv) and the quality of evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child thinks about where online information comes from and checks if the evidence is trustworthy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an argument, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use adequate volume and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can give a clear oral report or argument by organizing ideas, using facts and details, and speaking loudly and clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker’s argument and tell which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child understands information shown in different ways, like pictures, numbers, or speech, and explains how it relates to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts with peers by following agreed upon rules for collegial discussions, setting specific goals, and carrying out assigned roles; making comments and posing and responding to specific questions with elaboration and detail; and demonstrating understanding of various perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child takes part in group talks about grade-level topics by following rules, sharing ideas, asking questions, and understanding others’ views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write by hand or with technology to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of two pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child writes and publishes work by hand or on a computer and can type at least two pages in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach appropriate to audience and purpose. (Editing should demonstrate command of grade-level Grammar and Conventions.)
description_plain: With help, your child plans, revises, edits, and rewrites their writing to make it better for their audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent organizational structures of multiple paragraphs in which facts and ideas are logically grouped; headings, as applicable are included to support the purpose; and words, phrases, and clauses clarify the relationships between and among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child writes organized paragraphs with facts and ideas grouped logically and uses headings and clear words to show connections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional narratives that establish a situation and narrator; engage and orient the reader to the context; use narrative techniques such as description, dialogue, pacing, concrete words and sensory details to develop the characters, event(s), or experience(s); and provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated event(s).
description_plain: Your child writes stories that set the scene and narrator, use details like dialogue and description, and end with a clear conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informational texts that introduce the topic, develop the focus with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, and examples from multiple sources using appropriate strategies, such as description, comparison, and/or cause-effect; and provide a concluding section that follows from the information presented.
description_plain: Your child writes informational texts that introduce a topic, include facts and examples from different sources, and end with a summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that introduce and support a distinct point of view with relevant claims, evidence and reasoning; demonstrate an understanding of the topic; and provide a concluding section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that clearly state a point of view, support it with reasons and evidence, and finish with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely engaging in the production of shorter and longer pieces for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences. This could include, among others, summaries, reflections, descriptions, critiques, letters, and poetry, etc.
description_plain: Your child practices writing different types of pieces, both short and long, for various purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world. (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.)
description_plain: Your child reads several texts on related topics to learn more about the world, choosing books they can read alone or with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct brief as well as multi-day research tasks to take some action or share findings orally or in writing by formulating research questions and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate; gathering and assessing the relevance and usefulness of information from multiple reliable sources; and paraphrasing or quoting the data and conclusions of others, providing basic bibliographic information for sources, and respecting copyright guidelines for use of images.
description_plain: Your child does short or longer research projects by asking questions, finding useful information from trusted sources, and sharing what they learn while giving credit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and content-specific words and phrases occurring in grade-level reading and content; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression. Use these words in discussions and writing.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words from their reading and uses these words when talking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning, tone (author's attitude toward the subject), or mood (emotional atmosphere).
description_plain: Your child thinks about how a word choice affects the meaning, feeling, or mood of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., house versus home, cheap versus affordable).
description_plain: Your child notices the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like the difference between house and home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like cause and effect or part and whole, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language (e.g., personification, idioms) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language like idioms and personification by looking at how they are used in context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words and phrases provide meaning and nuance to grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child figures out how words and phrases add meaning and subtle differences to texts they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), print or digital, to find the pronunciation of a word and determine and clarify its precise meaning and its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say a word, what it means exactly, and its part of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., in readings on pioneers of space, determine the meanings of the words astronaut and nautical).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence or paragraph to understand the meaning of unknown words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child uses different strategies to figure out or clarify the meaning of new or multiple-meaning words in grade-level reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another (e.g., a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).
description_plain: Your child compares how two authors tell the same story differently, like a memoir versus a biography.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace the argument and specific claims in texts, distinguishing claims that are supported by evidence and reasons from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child follows an argument in a text and tells which claims have good evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a specific sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of texts and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child explains how a sentence, paragraph, or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in texts through examples or anecdotes.
description_plain: Your child explains in detail how a key person, event, or idea is introduced and explained in a text using examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain stated or implied central ideas from texts, including how they are developed using specific details from the texts; provide a summary of texts distinct from personal opinions.
description_plain: Your child explains the main ideas in a text using details and can summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from nonfiction works to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from nonfiction texts to show they understand what they read at grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences between stories and poems or different types of stories by looking at how they handle the same ideas or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how authors develop the point of view of the narrator or speaker in texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how authors show the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of texts and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the whole story and helps develop the main idea, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters act or change as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain stated or implied themes of texts, including how they are developed using specific details from the texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main message or theme of a story and show how details in the text help develop that theme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from literature to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child uses examples from books to show they understand what they read at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and expression in successive readings to support comprehension
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books accurately and with good speed and expression to help understand the story better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level texts to support claims and inferences, including quoting and paraphrasing from texts accurately.
description_plain: Your child finds several pieces of evidence from books to support their ideas, including quoting or putting information in their own words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in a volume of reading, independently, with peers, or with modest support related to the topics and themes being studied to build knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child reads a lot on their own or with others about topics they are studying to learn more and build vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend texts representing a balance of genres, cultures, and perspectives that exhibit complexity at the lower end of the grades 6–8 band.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands different kinds of books from various cultures and perspectives that are suitable for grades 6 to 8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell derivatives correctly by applying knowledge of bases and affixes.
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly by understanding word parts like roots and endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie).
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly to separate two or more adjectives that describe the same noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas, parentheses, and dashes set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, and dashes to set off extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to create meaning.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to make their meaning clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, or reduce sentences (e.g., adding or deleting modifiers, combining, or breaking up sentences) for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer or shorter and combine or break them up to make their writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child chooses different types of sentences to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses correctly within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child puts phrases and clauses in the right place in sentences and fixes mistakes where words are unclear or misplaced.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what phrases and clauses do in sentences and can explain their role.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the eight basic parts of speech (noun, pronoun, verb, adverb, adjective, conjunction, preposition, interjection).
description_plain: Your child can identify the eight main parts of speech like nouns, verbs, adjectives, and others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include digital components in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child adds digital tools like images or videos to presentations to help explain their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a text to an audio, video, or digital version of the text, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject.
description_plain: Your child compares a text with its audio, video, or digital version and explains how each shows the topic differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consider the reliability of websites and blog posts through such means as determining if they are run by established institutions, have named expertise, link to other reputable websites, and are current.
description_plain: Your child learns how to tell if websites or blogs are trustworthy by checking who runs them, their experts, links, and how current they are.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in positive, safe, legal, and ethical behavior when using information and communication technologies, including social interactions online or when using networked devices.
description_plain: Your child uses technology and online tools safely, legally, and respectfully, including when talking with others online.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an argument, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate vocabulary volume and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child gives clear oral reports or arguments on a topic, using important facts and speaking with the right volume and clear words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child explains a speaker's argument and checks if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child looks at main ideas and details in different types of media and explains how they help understand a topic or issue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts by following rules for collegial discussions, defining individual roles, and setting specific goals; posing questions that elicit elaboration and responding to others with relevant observations; and acknowledging new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modifying their own views.
description_plain: Your child takes part in group discussions by following rules, asking questions, sharing ideas, and changing their views when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write by hand or with technology to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child writes and publishes work by hand or on a computer, including linking to sources and working with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. (Editing should demonstrate command of grade-level Grammar and Conventions.)
description_plain: With help from adults or friends, your child plans, revises, edits, or rewrites their writing to make it better for the audience and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent organizational structures in which ideas and other information are logically grouped; headings and other formatting support the purpose; and precise language, content-specific vocabulary, and appropriate transitions create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child organizes their writing clearly with logical groups, headings, and smooth transitions using precise words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional narratives that establish a situation and narrator; engage and orient the reader to the context and point of view; use narrative techniques such as description, dialogue, pacing and a variety of precise words and transitional words and phrases to develop the characters, convey sequence, and signal shifts from one timeframe or setting to another; and provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated event(s).
description_plain: Your child writes stories that set the scene and narrator, use description and dialogue, show events in order, and end with a clear conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informational texts that introduce the topic clearly; develop the focus with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples from multiple sources using strategies such as description, enumeration, classification, comparison, problem-solution, and/or cause-effect; and provide a concluding section that follows from the information presented.
description_plain: Your child writes informational texts that clearly introduce a topic, give facts and examples from different sources, and end with a fitting conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments that introduce and support a well-defined point of view with appropriate claims, relevant evidence and clear reasoning, demonstrate a keen understanding of the topic or text, and provide a concluding section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that clearly state their opinion, support it with evidence and reasons, and finish with a strong conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely engaging in the production of shorter and longer pieces for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences. This could include, among others, summaries, reflections, descriptions, critiques, letters, and poetry, etc.
description_plain: Your child practices writing different types of pieces, both short and long, for various purposes and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world. (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.)
description_plain: Your child reads several texts about related topics to learn more about the world, choosing books they can read alone or with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct brief as well as multi-day research tasks to take some action or share findings orally or in writing by formulating research questions and generating additional questions for further research; gathering and assessing the relevance and usefulness of information from multiple reliable sources; and summarizing, paraphrasing, or quoting the data and conclusions of others, avoiding plagiarism, and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child does short or longer research projects by asking questions, finding and checking information from reliable sources, and sharing what they learn without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and content-specific words and phrases occurring in grade-level reading and content; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression. Use these words in discussions and writing.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new academic and subject-specific words from their reading and uses these words when talking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning, tone, or mood, including the impact of repeated use of certain images.
description_plain: Your child thinks about how a writer’s choice of words affects the meaning, feeling, or mood of a text, including when certain images are repeated.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., curious versus nosy, assertive versus pushy).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings, like curious versus nosy or assertive versus pushy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language (e.g., euphemism, oxymoron) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figurative language, like euphemisms or oxymorons, by looking at how they are used in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words and phrases provide meaning and nuance to grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child notices how words and phrases add meaning and subtle differences to texts they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the context or using a dictionary to be sure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), print or digital, to find the pronunciation of a grade-level word and determine or clarify its precise meaning and its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child looks up words in dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, either in books or online, to find how to say them, what they mean exactly, and what part of speech they are.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., in readings about earth sciences, determine the meanings of the words geologist and geophysics).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to figure out the meaning of new words, like geologist or geophysics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence or paragraph to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child figures out or clears up the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using different helpful strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child compares how different authors write about the same topic by looking at what facts they focus on or how they explain things differently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace the argument and specific claims in texts and assess whether the evidence is sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child follows the main argument and claims in a text and decides if the evidence given really supports those claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more texts and analyze how the differing structure of each text contributes to its meaning and development of ideas.
description_plain: Your child compares how two or more texts are organized and explains how their different structures help develop the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationships or interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in texts (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child looks at how people, events, and ideas in a text affect each other and interact.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain stated or implied central ideas of texts, analyzing their development over the course of texts; provide objective summaries of texts.
description_plain: Your child explains the main ideas in a text, how they grow throughout the reading, and summarizes the text without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from nonfiction works to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child uses facts and details from nonfiction texts to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast fictional portrayals of a time, place, or character and historical accounts of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child compares made-up stories about a time or place with real historical accounts to see how fiction writers change or use history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how authors develop and contrast the point of view of different characters or narrators in texts.
description_plain: Your child explains how authors show different points of view through characters or narrators.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the structure of two or more stories, poems, and plays and analyze how the differing structure of each literary text contributes to its meaning and style.
description_plain: Your child compares how stories, poems, or plays are organized and explains how their structure affects their meaning and style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how particular elements of stories or dramas interact including how setting shapes the characters or plot.
description_plain: Your child explains how parts of stories or plays work together, like how the setting influences the characters or the plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain stated or implied themes, analyzing their development over the course of texts; provide objective summaries of literary texts.
description_plain: Your child explains the main themes in stories or plays, how they develop, and summarizes the texts without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from literature to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child uses examples from literature to show they understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with accuracy, automaticity, appropriate rate, and expression in successive readings to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts smoothly, accurately, and with expression to help understand the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw several pieces of evidence from grade-level texts to support claims and inferences, including quoting or paraphrasing from texts accurately and tracing where in texts relevant evidence is located.
description_plain: Your child finds several pieces of evidence in texts to support their ideas and can quote or explain where they found the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Regularly engage in a volume of reading, independently, with peers, or with modest support related to the topics and themes being studied to build knowledge and vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child reads regularly on their own, with friends, or with some help about topics they are studying to build knowledge and learn new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend texts representing a balance of genres, cultures, and perspectives that exhibit complexity at the midrange of the grades 6–8 band.
description_plain: Your child reads and understands different kinds of texts from various cultures and viewpoints that are appropriate for grades 6 to 8.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell derivatives correctly by applying knowledge of bases and affixes.
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly by using what they know about word parts like bases and endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas, ellipsis, and dashes when writing and reading aloud to indicate a pause, break, or omission.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, ellipsis, and dashes correctly when writing or reading aloud to show pauses, breaks, or missing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to create meaning.
description_plain: Your child uses correct punctuation and capitalization when writing and reading aloud to make their meaning clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when it is needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, or reduce sentences (e.g., adding or deleting modifiers, combining, or breaking up sentences) for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child changes sentences by adding or removing details or combining and splitting sentences to make their writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of verbals (gerunds, participles, infinitives) in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands how verb forms like gerunds, participles, and infinitives work in sentences and can explain their use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voice to achieve particular effects.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs in active and passive voice to create different effects in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the indicative, imperative, interrogative, and conditional mood.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs in different moods like statements, commands, questions, and conditions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb voice and mood.
description_plain: Your child notices and fixes when verb voice or mood changes in a way that doesn’t fit the sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate digital displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child adds digital images or charts to presentations to make information clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of using different mediums—print or digital text—to present a particular topic or idea.
description_plain: Your child thinks about the pros and cons of using print or digital texts to share information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consider the evidence websites or blog posts use to support their position (e.g., Are they transparent about their sources? Do they link to peer-reviewed articles?).
description_plain: Your child checks if websites or blogs show good evidence for their opinions, like sharing sources or linking to trustworthy articles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate an understanding of and respect for the rights and obligations of using and sharing intellectual property.
description_plain: Your child understands and respects the rules about using and sharing other people’s creative work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report orally on a topic or text or present an argument, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, and well-chosen details; use appropriate vocabulary, volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child gives clear oral reports or arguments on a topic, focusing on important points with good evidence, using the right words, volume, and clear speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker's argument, check if the reasons and evidence make sense, and notice when unrelated information is added.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the intent (e.g., social, political, commercial) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can look at information in different forms like pictures, numbers, or speech, and understand why it was shared, such as for social, political, or business reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage in collaborative discussions about grade-level topics and texts with peers by following rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, defining individual roles, and tracking progress on specific goals; propelling conversations forward by posing and responding to questions, relating the current discussion to broader themes, and connecting the ideas of several speakers; and when warranted, qualifying or justifying one's views considering new evidence heard.
description_plain: Your child will join group talks about grade-level topics by following discussion rules, taking roles, asking and answering questions, connecting ideas, and changing their opinion when new facts are shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write by hand or with technology to produce and publish writing, link to and cite sources, present the relationships between information and ideas efficiently, and interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can write and share their work by hand or on a computer, include sources, show how ideas connect clearly, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With support from adults and peers, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed. (Editing should demonstrate command of grade-level Grammar and Conventions.)
description_plain: With help from adults or friends, your child will plan, revise, edit, or rewrite their writing to make it better for the audience and purpose, using correct grammar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent organizational structures in which ideas and other information are logically grouped; headings, other formatting, and graphics (e.g., charts and tables) support the purpose; and precise language, content-specific vocabulary, and varied transitions create cohesion and clarify the relationships between and among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child can organize their writing clearly with logical groups of ideas, use headings and pictures to help, and choose exact words and transitions to make ideas easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write personal or fictional narratives that establish a situation and narrator; engage and orient the reader to the context and one or multiple points of view; use a variety of techniques such as description, dialogue, pacing, and precise words and phrases, sensory language and transition words to develop the characters, capture the action and convey sequence, and signal shifts from one timeframe or setting to another; and provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated event(s).
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events that set the scene and narrator, use description, dialogue, and sensory details to develop characters and action, show time changes, and end with a fitting conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informational texts that introduce the topic clearly; preview what is to follow by establishing and maintaining a clear focus with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, and examples from multiple sources using appropriate strategies, such as description, enumeration, classification, comparison, problem-solution, and/or cause-effect; and provide a concluding section that follows from the information presented.
description_plain: Your child can write clear informational texts that introduce a topic, stay focused with facts and examples from different sources, use strategies like comparison or cause-effect, and finish with a strong ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments or make claims that support well-defined points of view effectively with relevant evidence and clear reasoning in ways that logically advance the claim(s) made; demonstrate a nuanced understanding of the topic; and provide a concluding section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that clearly state their point, support it with good reasons and evidence, show a deep understanding, and end with a conclusion that supports their claim.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop flexibility in writing by routinely engaging in the production of shorter and longer pieces for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences. This could include, among others, summaries, reflections, descriptions, critiques, letters, and poetry, etc.
description_plain: Your child will practice writing different kinds of pieces, both short and long, for various purposes and audiences, like summaries, letters, poems, or reflections.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read a series of texts organized around a variety of conceptually related topics to build knowledge about the world. (These texts should be at a range of complexity levels so students can read the texts independently, with peers, or with modest support.)
description_plain: Your child will read several texts on related topics to learn more about the world, choosing books they can read alone, with friends, or with some help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct brief as well as multi-day research tasks to take some action or share findings orally or in writing by formulating research questions and generating additional questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration; gathering and assessing the relevance and credibility of information from multiple sources; and summarizing, paraphrasing, or quoting the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citations.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects by asking questions, finding trustworthy information from many sources, and sharing what they learn without copying, using proper citations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and content-specific words and phrases occurring in grade-level reading and content; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression. Use these words in discussions and writing.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use new academic and subject-specific words they find in their reading and use these words when talking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning and tone, including analogies or allusions to other texts.
description_plain: Your child can understand how specific word choices affect the meaning and feeling of a text, including comparisons or references to other works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., crowd versus mob, fired versus laid off).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings, like 'crowd' versus 'mob' or 'fired' versus 'laid off.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., homonyms, person to location, object to use) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationship between words, like words that sound the same or how objects relate to their uses, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language (e.g., verbal irony, puns) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand figurative language like jokes, puns, or irony by looking at the context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine how words and phrases provide meaning and nuance to texts.
description_plain: Your child understands how words and phrases add meaning and subtle differences to texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary.
description_plain: Your child can check if their guess about a word’s meaning fits by looking at the sentence or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), print or digital, to find the pronunciation of a grade-level word and determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say a word, what it means exactly, or its part of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., in readings about mathematics, determine the meanings of the words percentile and perimeter).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to figure out the meaning of new words, like 'percentile' or 'perimeter.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence or paragraph to understand the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade-level content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze cases in which two or more texts provide conflicting information on the same topic and identify where the texts disagree on matters of fact or interpretation.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more texts that disagree on the same topic and point out where they differ in facts or opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace the argument and specific claims in texts and assess whether all the evidence presented is relevant and whether irrelevant evidence was introduced.
description_plain: Your child can follow the argument and claims in a text and decide if the evidence is relevant or if unrelated information is included.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structural elements of a text, including the role of specific sentences, paragraphs, and text features in developing and refining key concepts.
description_plain: Your child can look at how a text is put together, including sentences, paragraphs, and features, to understand key ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how texts make connections among and distinctions between individuals, ideas, or events (e.g., through comparisons, analogies, or categories).
description_plain: Your child can see how texts connect or separate people, ideas, or events using comparisons, categories, or analogies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain stated or implied central ideas of texts, analyzing their development over the course of the texts, including the relationship of individuals, ideas, or events to the central ideas; provide objective summaries of texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main ideas in a text, how they develop, and how people, ideas, or events relate to those ideas, and can summarize the text fairly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use evidence from nonfiction works to demonstrate understanding of grade-level texts.
description_plain: Your child can use evidence from nonfiction texts to show they understand grade-level reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Relate themes, patterns of events, or character types from myths, traditional stories, or religious works to contemporary stories, poems, or drama.
description_plain: Your child can connect themes or character types from myths or traditional stories to modern stories, poems, or plays.

subject: Language Arts
