description_plain: Your child can explain how different points of view between characters and the audience, like in dramatic irony, create feelings like suspense or humor.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how authors structure texts to advance a plot, explaining how each event gives rise to the next or foreshadows a future event.
description_plain: Your child can explain how authors organize stories to move the plot forward, showing how one event leads to another or hints at what will happen.

description: Analyze how characters are revealed through particular lines of dialogue or events in literary texts.
description_plain: Your child can explain how characters are shown through their dialogue or actions in stories.

description_plain: Your child can explain the themes in stories, how they develop, and how characters, setting, and plot relate to those themes.

description_plain: Your child can use evidence from literature to show they understand grade-level reading.

description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts accurately, smoothly, and with expression to help understand what they read.

description_plain: Your child can find several pieces of evidence from texts that support what is said directly and what is implied, including quoting and paraphrasing with correct citations.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description: Starting at a given number, count forward within 100 and backward within 20.
description_plain: Your child can start at any number and count forward up to 100 and backward up to 20.

subject: Math
description: Count to 100 by ones and by tens.
description_plain: Your child can count to 100 by ones and by tens.

subject: Math
description: Know number names and the count sequence.
description_plain: Your child knows number names and the order of counting.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Describe the whole as two of, or four of, the shares.
description_plain: Your child can explain that the whole shape is made up of two or four equal parts.

subject: Math
description: Describe the shares using the words "halves," "fourths," and "quarters," and use the phrases "half of," "a fourth of," and "a quarter of."
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'halves,' 'fourths,' and 'quarters' to describe parts of a shape and say phrases like 'half of' or 'a quarter of.'

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares. Understand for these examples that decomposing into more equal shares creates smaller shares.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two or four equal parts and understands that more parts mean smaller pieces.

subject: Math
description: Compose two-dimensional (rectangles, squares, trapezoids, triangles, half-circles, and quarter-circles) or three-dimensional shapes (cubes, right rectangular prisms, right circular cones, and right circular cylinders) to create a composite shape, and compose new shapes from the composite shape.
description_plain: Your child can put together flat shapes like squares and triangles or solid shapes like cubes and cones to make new shapes.

subject: Math
description: Compare defining attributes and non-defining attributes of two- and three-dimensional shapes; build and draw shapes that possess defining attributes.
description_plain: Your child can compare important features of flat and solid shapes and can build or draw shapes using those features.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can think about shapes and their features.

subject: Math
description: Identify quarters, dimes, and nickels and relate their values to pennies. Find equivalent values (e.g., a nickel is equivalent to five pennies).
description_plain: Your child can recognize quarters, dimes, and nickels, know how much they are worth compared to pennies, and find coins that equal the same amount.

subject: Math
description: Work with money.
description_plain: Work with money.

subject: Math
description: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories; ask and answer questions about the total number of data points, how many in each category, and how many more or less are in one category than in another.
description_plain: Your child can collect and organize data into up to three groups and ask and answer questions about how many are in each group and how the groups compare.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the hour and half-hour using both analog and digital clocks.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time.
description_plain: Tell and write time.

subject: Math
description: Express the length of an object as a whole number of length units by laying multiple copies of a shorter object (the length unit) end to end; understand that the length measurement of an object is the number of same-size length units that span it with no gaps or overlaps.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long something is by lining up smaller objects end to end without gaps or overlaps and count how many it takes.

subject: Math
description: Order three objects by length; compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.
description_plain: Your child can put three objects in order from shortest to longest and compare two objects by using a third object.

subject: Math
description: Measure lengths indirectly and by iterating (repeating) length units.
description_plain: Your child can measure lengths by using repeated units or by comparing to other objects.

subject: Math
description: Subtract multiples of ten in the range 10 – 90 from multiples of ten in the range 10 – 90 by using physical, visual, and symbolic representations, with an emphasis on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationships between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can subtract multiples of ten between 10 and 90 from other multiples of ten using objects, pictures, or numbers and explain how they did it.

subject: Math
description: Given a two-digit number, mentally find ten more or ten less than the number, without having to count; explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can quickly find ten more or ten less than any two-digit number in their head and explain how they know.

subject: Math
description: Understand that when adding two-digit numbers, combine like base-ten units such as tens and tens, ones and ones, and sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.
description_plain: Your child understands that when adding two-digit numbers, they add tens to tens and ones to ones, and sometimes need to make a new ten.

subject: Math
description: Add a two-digit number and a multiple of ten.
description_plain: Your child can add a two-digit number and a multiple of ten.

subject: Math
description: Add a two-digit number and a one-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can add a two-digit number and a one-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Add whole numbers within 100 by using physical, visual, and symbolic representations, with an emphasis on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add numbers up to 100 using objects, pictures, or numbers, focusing on place value and how addition and subtraction relate.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
description_plain: Your child can use their understanding of tens and ones to add and subtract numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.
description_plain: Your child can compare two two-digit numbers by looking at the tens and ones, and use symbols like >, =, and < to show which is bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: The numbers 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80, 90 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine tens (and zero ones).
description_plain: Your child understands that numbers like 10, 20, 30, etc., mean groups of tens and zero ones.

subject: Math
description: The numbers from 11 to 19 are composed of a ten and one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine ones.
description_plain: Your child knows that numbers from 11 to 19 are made of one ten and some ones.

subject: Math
description: 10 can be thought of as a bundle of ten ones — called a "ten."
description_plain: Your child understands that 10 ones can be grouped together to make one 'ten.'

subject: Math
description: Understand that the two digits of a two-digit number represent amounts of tens and ones. Understand:
description_plain: Your child understands that a two-digit number has tens and ones, and what each digit means.

subject: Math
description: Understand place value.
description_plain: Understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Starting at a given number, count forward and backwards within 120 by ones. Skip count by twos to 20, by fives to 100, and by tens to 120. In this range, read and write numerals and represent a number of objects with a written numeral.
description_plain: Your child can count forward and backward by ones up to 120, skip count by twos, fives, and tens, read and write numbers, and match numbers to groups of objects.

subject: Math
description: Extend the counting sequence.
description_plain: Extend the counting sequence.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating three whole numbers, with the unknown in any position.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the missing number in an addition or subtraction problem with three numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand the meaning of the equal sign, and determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.
description_plain: Your child understands what the equal sign means and can tell if addition or subtraction equations are true or false.

subject: Math
description: Work with addition and subtraction equations.
description_plain: Your child can work with addition and subtraction equations.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency for addition and subtraction within ten, use strategies to add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to ten and use strategies to add and subtract numbers up to twenty.

subject: Math
description: Relate counting to addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can use counting to help with adding and subtracting.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Add and subtract within 20.

subject: Math
description: Restate a subtraction problem as a missing addend problem using the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can change a subtraction problem into an addition problem to find the missing number.

subject: Math
description: Apply properties of operations to add.
description_plain: Your child can use rules about numbers to help add more easily.

subject: Math
description: Understand and apply properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses the rules for adding and subtracting, and knows how they are connected.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20 by using physical, visual, and symbolic representations.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that add three numbers with a total of 20 or less using objects, drawings, or equations with a missing number.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction word problems within 20 involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, by using physical, visual, and symbolic representations.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple addition and subtraction story problems up to 20 using objects, pictures, or numbers.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can show and solve problems using addition and subtraction.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that equal shares of identical wholes need not have the same shape.
description_plain: Your child understands that equal parts of the same whole can look different in shape.

subject: Math
description: Describe the whole as two of, three of, or four of the shares.
description_plain: Your child can explain that the whole is made up of two, three, or four equal parts.

subject: Math
description: Describe the shares using the words "halves," "thirds," "fourths," and "quarter," and use the phrases "half of," "a third of," "a fourth of," and "quarter of."
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'halves,' 'thirds,' 'fourths,' and 'quarters' to describe parts of a shape and say phrases like 'half of' or 'a third of.'

subject: Math
description: Partition circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal shares. Understand for these examples that decomposing into more equal shares creates smaller shares.
description_plain: Your child can divide circles and rectangles into two, three, or four equal parts and knows that more parts mean smaller pieces.

subject: Math
description: Partition a rectangle into rows and columns of same-size squares and count to find the total number of them.
description_plain: Your child can divide a rectangle into equal squares by making rows and columns and count how many squares there are.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and draw shapes having specified attributes, such as a given number of angles or a given number of equal faces. Identify triangles, squares, rectangles, rhombi, trapezoids, pentagons, hexagons, octagons, and cubes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and draw shapes by their features, like number of sides or equal faces, and name shapes like triangles and cubes.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
description_plain: Your child understands shapes and their features and can think about how they are alike and different.

subject: Math
description: Draw a picture graph and a bar graph (with single-unit scale) to represent a data set with up to four categories. Solve simple put-together, take-apart, and compare problems using information presented in the graph.
description_plain: Your child can make picture and bar graphs with up to four categories and use them to solve simple problems.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of several objects to the nearest whole unit, or by making repeated measurements of the same object. Organize and record data on a line plot (dot plot) where the horizontal scale is marked off in whole-number units.
description_plain: Your child can measure several objects, record the lengths on a line plot, and organize the data.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving dollar bills, quarters, dimes, nickels, and pennies (up to $10), using $ and ¢ symbols appropriately and whole-dollar amounts.
description_plain: Your child can solve money problems using dollars and coins up to $10 and use the correct symbols for dollars and cents.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time from analog and digital clocks to the nearest five minutes, using a.m. and p.m.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest five minutes on analog and digital clocks, using a.m. and p.m.

subject: Math
description: Work with time and money.
description_plain: Work with time and money.

subject: Math
description: Represent whole numbers as lengths from zero on a number line diagram with equally spaced points corresponding to the numbers 0, 1, 2, ..., and represent whole-number sums and differences within 100 on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child can show whole numbers on a number line and use the line to add and subtract numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve word problems involving lengths that are given in the same units.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems about lengths using addition and subtraction within 100, with drawings or equations.

subject: Math
description: Relate addition and subtraction to length.
description_plain: Your child understands how addition and subtraction relate to measuring length.

subject: Math
description: Measure to determine how much longer one object is than another, expressing the length difference in terms of a standard length unit.
description_plain: Your child can measure how much longer one object is than another using standard units.

subject: Math
description: Estimate lengths using units of inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.
description_plain: Your child can estimate lengths using inches, feet, centimeters, and meters.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object twice, using length units of different lengths for the two measurements; describe how the two measurements relate to the size of the unit chosen.
description_plain: Your child can measure something twice using different units and explain how the measurements change with the unit size.

subject: Math
description: Measure the length of an object by selecting and using appropriate tools such as rulers, yardsticks, meter sticks, and measuring tapes.
description_plain: Your child can measure how long things are using tools like rulers or measuring tapes.

subject: Math
description: Measure and estimate lengths in standard units.
description_plain: Your child can measure and estimate lengths using standard units.

subject: Math
description: Explain why addition and subtraction strategies work, using place value and the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can explain why their ways of adding and subtracting work by using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Use mental strategies to add or subtract a number that is ten more, ten less, one hundred more, and one hundred less than a given three-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can mentally add or subtract numbers that are ten or one hundred more or less than a given three-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Understand that sometimes it is necessary to compose or decompose tens or hundreds.
description_plain: Your child understands that sometimes they need to break apart or put together tens or hundreds when adding or subtracting.

subject: Math
description: Understand that in adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, one adds or subtracts hundreds and hundreds, tens and tens, ones and ones.
description_plain: Your child knows that when adding or subtracting three-digit numbers, they add or subtract hundreds with hundreds, tens with tens, and ones with ones.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract whole numbers within 1,000, by using physical, visual, and symbolic representations, with an emphasis on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationships between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1,000 using objects, pictures, or numbers, focusing on place value and how addition and subtraction relate.

subject: Math
description: Add up to four two-digit numbers using strategies based on place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can add up to four two-digit numbers using place value and math properties.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract whole numbers within 100 using understanding of place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 100 using place value and number rules.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to add and subtract.
description_plain: Your child can use their understanding of place value to add and subtract numbers.

subject: Math
description: Compare two three-digit numbers based on meanings of the hundreds, tens, and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.
description_plain: Your child can compare two three-digit numbers by looking at their hundreds, tens, and ones digits and use >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Read and write numbers from 0 to 1,000 using standard form, expanded form, and word form.
description_plain: Your child can read and write numbers from 0 to 1,000 in normal form, expanded form, and words.

subject: Math
description: Count within 1,000; skip-count by fives, tens, and 100s. Identify patterns in skip counting starting at any number.
description_plain: Your child can count to 1,000, skip count by fives, tens, and hundreds, and notice patterns when skip counting from different numbers.

subject: Math
description: The numbers 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, and 900 refer to one, two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight, or nine hundreds (and 0 tens and 0 ones).
description_plain: Your child knows that numbers like 100, 200, 300, and so on mean one, two, three, etc. hundreds with zero tens and ones.

subject: Math
description: 100 can be thought of as a bundle of ten tens—called a "hundred."
description_plain: Your child understands that 100 is made by grouping ten tens together, which is called a 'hundred.'

subject: Math
description: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent amounts of hundreds, tens, and ones. Understand:
description_plain: Your child understands that a three-digit number shows how many hundreds, tens, and ones it has, like 706 means 7 hundreds, 0 tens, and 6 ones.

subject: Math
description: Understand place value.
description_plain: Understand place value.

subject: Math
description: Use addition to find the total number of objects arranged in rectangular arrays with up to 5 rows and up to 5 columns; write an equation to express the total as a sum of equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can use addition to find out how many objects are in a rectangle with up to 5 rows and 5 columns, and can write an equation to show this.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a group of objects (up to 20) has an odd or even number of members and write an equation to express an even number as a sum of two equal addends.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a group of up to 20 objects has an odd or even number and write an equation showing an even number as two equal parts added together.

subject: Math
description: Work with equal groups of objects to gain foundations for multiplication.
description_plain: Your child works with equal groups of objects to start learning about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency for addition and subtraction within 20 using mental strategies. By the end of grade two, recall basic facts to add and subtract within 20 with automaticity.
description_plain: Your child can quickly add and subtract numbers up to 20 in their head and remember basic addition and subtraction facts.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract within 20.
description_plain: Add and subtract within 20.

subject: Math
description: Use addition and subtraction within 100 to solve one- and two-step word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, by using physical, visual, and symbolic representations.
description_plain: Your child can solve one- and two-step word problems using addition and subtraction within 100, drawing pictures and writing equations with unknown numbers.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can use addition and subtraction to solve different kinds of problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Partition two-dimensional figures into equal areas, and express the area of each part as a unit fraction of the whole.
description_plain: Your child can divide flat shapes into equal parts and say the size of each part as a fraction of the whole shape.

subject: Math
description: Understand that shapes in different categories may share attributes, and that the shared attributes can define a larger category. Compare and classify shapes by their sides and angles. Recognize rhombi, rectangles, squares, and trapezoids as examples of quadrilaterals, and draw examples of quadrilaterals that do not belong to any of these subcategories.
description_plain: Your child understands that shapes can share features and belong to bigger groups, can compare shapes by their sides and angles, and can recognize and draw different kinds of four-sided shapes.

subject: Math
description: Reason with shapes and their attributes.
description_plain: Your child can think about shapes and their features.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems involving perimeters of polygons, including finding the perimeter given the side lengths, finding an unknown side length, and exhibiting rectangles with the same perimeter and different areas or with the same area and different perimeters.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems about the perimeter of shapes by adding side lengths, finding missing sides, and exploring how shapes with the same perimeter can have different areas.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: Recognize perimeter as an attribute of plane figures and distinguish between linear and area measures.
description_plain: Your child knows that perimeter is the distance around a flat shape and can tell the difference between measuring length and measuring area.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as additive. Find areas of rectilinear figures by decomposing them into non-overlapping rectangles and adding the areas of the non-overlapping parts, applying this technique to solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of shapes made of rectangles by breaking them apart and adding the areas, and use this to solve real problems.

subject: Math
description: Use tiling to show in a concrete case that the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths a and b + c is the sum of a × b and a × c. Use area models to represent the distributive property in mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can use small squares to show that the area of a rectangle with sides a and b + c is the same as adding the areas of rectangles with sides a and b, and a and c.

subject: Math
description: Multiply side lengths to find areas of rectangles with whole-number side lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems, and represent whole-number products as rectangular areas in mathematical reasoning.
description_plain: Your child multiplies side lengths to find the area of rectangles and uses this to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with whole-number side lengths by tiling it, and show that the area is the same as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.
description_plain: Your child finds the area of a rectangle by counting squares or multiplying side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Relate area to the operations of multiplication and addition.
description_plain: Your child understands that area relates to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Measure areas by counting unit squares (square cm, square m, square in, square ft, and nonstandard units).
description_plain: Your child can measure area by counting how many square units fit inside a shape.

subject: Math
description: A plane figure which can be covered without gaps or overlaps by n unit squares is said to have an area of n square units.
description_plain: Your child knows that if a shape can be covered exactly by n squares, its area is n square units.

subject: Math
description: A square with side length one unit, called "a unit square," is said to have "one square unit" of area, and can be used to measure area.
description_plain: Your child knows that a square with sides one unit long is called a unit square and has an area of one square unit.

subject: Math
description: Recognize area as an attribute of plane figures and understand concepts of area measurement.
description_plain: Your child knows that area is a property of flat shapes and understands what it means to measure area.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: Understand concepts of area and relate area to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child understands what area means and knows that area can be found by multiplying or adding.

subject: Math
description: Generate measurement data by measuring lengths of objects using rulers marked with halves and fourths of an inch. Record and show the data by making a line plot (dot plot), where the horizontal scale is marked off in appropriate units— whole numbers, halves, or fourths.
description_plain: Your child can measure lengths using rulers marked in halves and fourths of an inch and make a line plot to show the measurements.

subject: Math
description: Draw a scaled picture graph and scaled bar graph to represent a data set with several categories. Solve one- and two-step "how many more" and "how many less" problems using information presented in scaled bar graphs.
description_plain: Your child can draw picture and bar graphs with a scale to show data and solve problems about how many more or less items there are.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Identify and use the appropriate tools and units of measurement, both customary and metric, to solve one-step word problems using the four operations involving weight, mass, liquid volume, and capacity (within the same system and unit).
description_plain: Your child can pick the right tools and units to measure weight, volume, and capacity and solve simple word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division.

subject: Math
description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute within the same hour and measure time intervals in minutes. Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes.
description_plain: Your child can tell and write time to the nearest minute, measure time intervals, and solve word problems about adding or subtracting time.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and estimation of intervals of time, liquid volumes, and masses of objects.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems involving measuring and estimating time, liquid amounts, and weights.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with the same numerator or the same denominator by reasoning about their size. Recognize the comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole. Record the results of the comparisons with the symbols >, = , and <, and justify the conclusion using visual representations and/or verbal reasoning.
description_plain: Your child compares two fractions with the same top or bottom number by thinking about their size and uses >, =, or < to show which is bigger or if they are equal.

subject: Math
description: Express whole numbers as fractions, and recognize fractions that are equivalent to whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write whole numbers as fractions and recognize when fractions are equal to whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Recognize and generate simple equivalent fractions, and explain why the fractions are equivalent, such as by using a visual fraction model.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain simple fractions that are equal using pictures or models.

subject: Math
description: Understand two fractions as equivalent (equal) if they are the same size, or the same point on a number line.
description_plain: Your child understands that two fractions are equal if they represent the same amount or point on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Explain equivalence of fractions and compare fractions by reasoning about their size, in limited cases.
description_plain: Your child understands when fractions are equal and can compare their sizes in some cases.

subject: Math
description: Represent a fraction a/b on a number line diagram by marking off a length 1/b from 0. Recognize that the resulting interval has size a/b and that its endpoint locates the number a/b on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can show a fraction on a number line by marking parts and understand where the fraction fits on the line.

subject: Math
description: Represent a unit fraction 1/b on a number line diagram by defining the interval from 0 to 1 as the whole and partitioning it into b equal parts. Recognize that each part has size 1/b and that the fraction 1/b is located 1/b of a whole unit from 0 on the number line.
description_plain: Your child can divide a number line from 0 to 1 into equal parts to show a fraction and knows where that fraction is located.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction as a number on the number line; represent fractions on a number line diagram.
description_plain: Your child understands fractions as numbers and can place them correctly on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction 1/b as the quantity formed by one part when a whole (a single unit) is partitioned into b equal parts; understand a/b as the quantity formed by a parts of size 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction like 1/b means one part of a whole divided into b equal parts, and a/b means a parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of fractions as numbers.
description_plain: Your child learns that fractions are numbers and how they work.

subject: Math
description: Multiply one-digit whole numbers by multiples of ten in the range 10–90 using understanding of place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can multiply one-digit numbers by 10, 20, up to 90 using place value and math rules.

subject: Math
description: Fluently add and subtract whole numbers within 1,000 using understanding of place value and properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract numbers up to 1,000 quickly and correctly using place value understanding.

subject: Math
description: Round a whole number to the tens or hundreds place, using place value understanding or a visual representation.
description_plain: Your child can round numbers to the nearest ten or hundred using place value or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child uses their understanding of place value and math rules to solve problems with bigger numbers.

subject: Math
description: Identify arithmetic patterns (including patterns in the addition table or multiplication table) and explain them using properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain patterns in addition and multiplication tables using math rules.

subject: Math
description: Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies, including rounding.
description_plain: Your child can check if answers make sense by estimating and rounding numbers.

subject: Math
description: Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity.
description_plain: Your child can write math problems as equations using letters for unknown numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve two-step word problems involving whole numbers using the four operations.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that need two steps using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving the four operations, and identify and explain patterns in arithmetic.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, and can find and explain patterns in numbers.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency for multiplication within 100. Know from memory all products of two single-digit numbers and related division facts.
description_plain: Your child can quickly recall multiplication facts for numbers up to 10 and related division facts.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate understanding of strategies that make use of the relationship between multiplication and division or properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child understands strategies that use the connection between multiplication and division or math properties.

subject: Math
description: Demonstrate fluency for multiplication within 100.
description_plain: Your child can multiply numbers up to 10 by 10 quickly and correctly.

subject: Math
description: Multiply and divide within 100.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide numbers up to 100.

subject: Math
description: Understand division as determining an unknown factor in a multiplication problem.
description_plain: Your child understands division as finding a missing number in a multiplication problem.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to multiply and divide.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to multiply and divide numbers.

subject: Math
description: Understand properties of multiplication and the relationship between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child understands how multiplication works and how it is connected to division.

subject: Math
description: Determine the unknown whole number in a multiplication or division equation relating three whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can find the missing number in multiplication or division problems without needing special math words.

subject: Math
description: Use multiplication and division within 100 to solve word problems involving equal groups, arrays, and measurements by using visual and symbolic representations, with a symbol for an unknown number.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems about equal groups, arrays, and measurements using multiplication and division, and use symbols for unknown numbers.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a quotient of whole numbers as equal sharing, e.g., 56 ÷ 8 as the number in each share when 56 objects are split into 8 equal shares, or as the number of shares when 56 objects are split into equal shares of 8 objects each.
description_plain: Your child can understand division as sharing equally or grouping equally and explain what the numbers mean.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a product of whole numbers as a grouping of sets, e.g., 5 × 7 as five groups of seven objects each.
description_plain: Your child can understand multiplication as making groups of the same size and explain what the numbers mean.

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems using multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a line of symmetry for a two-dimensional figure as a line across the figure such that the figure can be folded along the line into matching parts. Identify line-symmetric figures and draw lines of symmetry.
description_plain: Your child can find and draw lines of symmetry on flat shapes by folding them to see if both sides match.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures based on the presence or absence of parallel or perpendicular lines, or the presence or absence of angles of a specified size. Recognize right triangles as a category, and identify right triangles.
description_plain: Your child can sort flat shapes by looking for parallel or perpendicular lines and by naming triangles as right, acute, or obtuse based on their angles.

subject: Math
description: Draw points, lines, line segments, rays, angles (right, acute, obtuse), and perpendicular and parallel lines. Identify these in two-dimensional figures.
description_plain: Your child can draw points, lines, line segments, rays, and different types of angles, and find these parts in flat shapes.

subject: Math
description: Draw and identify lines and angles, and classify shapes by properties of their lines and angles.
description_plain: Your child can draw and identify lines and angles and group shapes based on their lines and angles.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angle measure as additive. When an angle is decomposed into non-overlapping parts, the angle measure of the whole is the sum of the angle measures of the parts.
description_plain: Your child knows that the size of a whole angle is the sum of the sizes of its parts when the angle is split.

subject: Math
description: Use an equation with a symbol for the unknown angle measure.
description_plain: Your child can write an equation using a symbol to find a missing angle measure.

subject: Math
description: Solve addition and subtraction problems to find unknown angles on a diagram in real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems to find missing angles by adding or subtracting angle measures in pictures or real life.

subject: Math
description: Measure angles in whole-number degrees using a protractor. Sketch angles of specified measure.
description_plain: Your child can measure angles using a protractor and draw angles with a given size.

subject: Math
description: An angle that turns through n one-degree angles is said to have an angle measure of n degrees.
description_plain: Your child knows that an angle made by turning through n one-degree steps measures n degrees.

subject: Math
description: An angle is measured with reference to a circle with its center at the common endpoint of the rays, by considering the fraction of the circular arc between the points where the two rays intersect the circle.
description_plain: Your child understands that an angle is measured by how much it turns around a point, with one degree being 1/360 of a full turn.

subject: Math
description: Recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint, and understand concepts of angle measurement.
description_plain: Your child knows that angles are shapes made by two rays sharing a point and learns how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: Understand concepts of angle and measure angles.
description_plain: Your child learns about angles and how to measure them.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot (dot plot) to show a set of measurements in fractions of a unit ½, ¼, 1/8. Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots (dot plots).
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot to show measurements in fractions like halves, quarters, and eighths, and solve addition and subtraction problems using the plot.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Represent measurement quantities using diagrams such as number line diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures like number lines with measurement marks to show measurement amounts.

subject: Math
description: Include problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that change measurements from bigger units to smaller units.

subject: Math
description: Include problems involving simple fractions or decimals.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that include simple fractions or decimals in measurements.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations to solve word problems involving measurements.
description_plain: Your child can use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division to solve word problems about measurements.

subject: Math
description: Record measurement equivalents in a two-column table.
description_plain: Your child can make a two-column table to show how different measurements are equal.

subject: Math
description: Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from bigger units into smaller units within the same system.

subject: Math
description: Know relative sizes of measurement units within any one system of units.
description_plain: Your child understands how different measurement units compare in size within one system.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems involving measurement and conversion of measurements from a larger unit to a smaller unit.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems that involve measuring things and changing measurements from larger units to smaller units.

subject: Math
description: Record the results of the comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <, and justify the conclusions using visual representations and/or verbal reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals using >, =, or < and explain why using pictures or words.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two decimals refer to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child understands that decimals can only be compared if they refer to the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Compare two decimals to hundredths by reasoning about their size.
description_plain: Your child can compare two decimals to the hundredths place by thinking about their size.

subject: Math
description: Use decimal notation to represent fractions with denominators 10 or 100.
description_plain: Your child can write fractions with denominators 10 or 100 as decimals.

subject: Math
description: Express a fraction with denominator 10 as an equivalent fraction with denominator 100, and use this technique to add two fractions with respective denominators 10 and 100.
description_plain: Your child can change a fraction with denominator 10 into an equal fraction with denominator 100 and use this to add fractions with denominators 10 and 100.

subject: Math
description: Understand decimal notation for fractions, and compare decimal fractions.
description_plain: Your child can understand how fractions and decimals are related and can compare decimal numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving multiplication of a fraction by a whole number e.g., by using visual fraction models and/or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that multiply a fraction by a whole number using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Understand a multiple of a/b as a multiple of 1/b, and use this understanding to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child understands that multiplying a fraction by a whole number means multiplying the numerator and can use models to show this.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b as a multiple of 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands a fraction like a/b as a number made up of a parts of size 1/b.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction by a whole number.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number and solve real-life problems using this skill.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions, including mixed numbers, with the same denominator. Justify the conclusions using a visual fraction model and/or verbal reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with the same denominator and explain their answers with pictures or words.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract mixed numbers with like denominators by replacing the mixed number with an equivalent fraction and/or by using properties of operations and the relationship between addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract mixed numbers with the same bottom number by changing them into improper fractions or using addition and subtraction rules.

subject: Math
description: Decompose a fraction into a sum of fractions with the same denominator in more than one way, recording each decomposition by an equation. Justify the conclusions by using a visual fraction model and/or verbal reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can break a fraction into smaller fractions with the same bottom number in different ways and explain their thinking using drawings or models.

subject: Math
description: Understand addition and subtraction of fractions as joining and separating parts referring to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child understands that adding and subtracting fractions means putting parts together or taking parts apart from the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Understand a fraction a/b with a > 1 as a sum of fractions 1/b.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction like a/b (where a is bigger than 1) is the same as adding several 1/b fractions.

subject: Math
description: Build fractions from unit fractions by applying and extending previous understandings of operations on whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can build fractions by adding unit fractions and use what they know about whole numbers to do this.

subject: Math
description: Record the results of comparisons with symbols >, =, or <, and justify the conclusions, by using a visual fraction model and/or verbal reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions using >, =, or < and explain why using pictures or words.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that comparisons are valid only when the two fractions refer to the same whole.
description_plain: Your child understands that fractions can only be compared if they are parts of the same whole.

subject: Math
description: Compare two fractions with different numerators and different denominators, by creating common denominators or numerators, or by comparing to a benchmark fraction such as ½.
description_plain: Your child can compare two fractions with different top and bottom numbers by finding common numbers or comparing them to a familiar fraction like one-half.

subject: Math
description: Explain why a fraction a/b is equivalent to a fraction n × a/n × b by using visual fraction models, with attention to how the numbers and sizes of the parts differ even though the two fractions themselves are the same size. Use this principle to recognize and generate equivalent fractions, including fractions greater than 1.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying the top and bottom of a fraction by the same number makes an equal fraction, using pictures, and can find and make equivalent fractions, even those bigger than 1.

subject: Math
description: Extend understanding of fraction equivalence and ordering.
description_plain: Your child understands how to tell if two fractions are equal and can put fractions in order from smallest to largest.

subject: Math
description: Illustrate and explain the calculation by using rectangular arrays, area models, and/or equations.
description_plain: Your child can show and explain division problems using pictures like arrays or area models and equations.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies based on place value, the properties of operations, and/or the relationship between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and math rules to help divide numbers.

subject: Math
description: Find whole-number quotients and remainders with up to four-digit dividends and one-digit divisors.
description_plain: Your child can divide numbers with up to four digits by one-digit numbers and find the quotient and remainder.

subject: Math
description: Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
description_plain: Your child can show and explain multiplication problems using equations, arrays, or area models.

subject: Math
description: Use strategies based on place value and the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and math rules to help multiply numbers.

subject: Math
description: Multiply a whole number of up to four digits by a one-digit whole number, and multiply two two-digit numbers.
description_plain: Your child can multiply a number with up to four digits by a one-digit number and multiply two two-digit numbers using place value and properties of multiplication, and explain their work with equations or models.

subject: Math
description: Fluently use the standard algorithm for multi-digit whole-number addition and subtraction.
description_plain: Your child can quickly and correctly add and subtract multi-digit whole numbers using the standard method.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding and properties of operations to perform multi-digit arithmetic on whole numbers less than or equal to 1,000,000.
description_plain: Your child uses place value and math rules to add, subtract, multiply, and divide whole numbers up to 1,000,000.

subject: Math
description: Use place value understanding or visual representation to round multi-digit whole numbers to any place.
description_plain: Your child can round multi-digit whole numbers to any place by using place value or pictures.

subject: Math
description: Read and write multi-digit whole numbers using standard form, expanded form, and word form. Compare two multi-digit numbers based on meanings of the digits and each place, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.
description_plain: Your child can read and write big numbers in normal form, expanded form, and words, and compare two numbers using >, =, or < by looking at each digit's value.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that in a multi-digit whole number, a digit in any place represents ten times as much as it represents in the place to its right.
description_plain: Your child understands that in a big number, a digit is worth ten times more than the digit to its right.

subject: Math
description: Generalize place value understanding for multi-digit whole numbers, less than or equal to 1,000,000.
description_plain: Your child understands place value for whole numbers up to 1,000,000.

subject: Math
description: Generate a number or shape pattern that follows a given rule. Identify and explain features of the pattern that were not explicit in the rule itself.
description_plain: Your child can make number or shape patterns that follow a rule and explain parts of the pattern that the rule doesn’t say.

subject: Math
description: Generate and analyze patterns.
description_plain: Your child can create and study patterns in numbers or shapes.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1–100 is prime or composite.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a whole number between 1 and 100 is prime or composite.

subject: Math
description: Determine whether a given whole number in the range 1–100 is a multiple of a given one-digit number.
description_plain: Your child can tell if a whole number between 1 and 100 is a multiple of a one-digit number.

subject: Math
description: Recognize that a whole number is a multiple of each of its factors.
description_plain: Your child knows that a whole number is a multiple of all its factors.

subject: Math
description: Find all factor pairs for a whole number in the range 1–100.
description_plain: Your child can find all pairs of factors for whole numbers between 1 and 100.

subject: Math
description: Gain familiarity with factors and multiples.
description_plain: Your child is getting to know how factors and multiples work.

subject: Math
description: Assess the reasonableness of answers using mental computation and estimation strategies, including rounding.
description_plain: Your child can check if answers make sense by using mental math and estimation like rounding.

subject: Math
description: Represent these problems using equations with a letter standing for the unknown quantity.
description_plain: Your child can write equations with letters for unknown numbers to show word problems.

subject: Math
description: Solve multi-step whole-number word problems using the four operations, including problems in which remainders must be interpreted.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step word problems with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division, including when there are remainders.

subject: Math
description: Multiply or divide to solve word problems involving multiplicative comparison.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems that compare amounts by multiplying or dividing.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a multiplication equation as a comparison, e.g., 35 = 5 × 7, as 35 is 5 times as many as 7. Represent verbal multiplicative comparisons as equations.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as a way to compare numbers and can write multiplication equations to show these comparisons.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations with whole numbers to solve problems.
description_plain: Your child can use addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division to solve different kinds of problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and express regularity in repeated reasoning.
description_plain: Your child can notice and explain patterns or repeated steps when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Look for and make use of structure.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the way math problems are put together to help solve them.

subject: Math
description: Attend to precision.
description_plain: Attend to precision.

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.
description_plain: Your child can choose and use the right tools, like calculators, rulers, or graphs, to help solve math problems.

subject: Math
description: Model with mathematics.
description_plain: Model with mathematics.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math thinking and listen to and question the ideas of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.
description_plain: Your child can think about math problems in different ways, using numbers and real-life examples.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.
description_plain: Your child can understand what a math problem is asking and keep working until they find a solution.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures in a hierarchy based on properties.
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes based on their properties in a way that shows which shapes belong to bigger or smaller groups.

subject: Math
description: Understand that attributes belonging to a category of two-dimensional figures also belong to all of the subcategories of that category.
description_plain: Your child understands that if a shape has certain features, all smaller groups of that shape also have those features.

subject: Math
description: Classify two-dimensional figures into categories based on their properties.
description_plain: Your child can group flat shapes into categories based on their features.

subject: Math
description: Represent real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in the first quadrant of the coordinate plane (x and y both have positive values), and interpret coordinate values of points in the context of the situation.
description_plain: Your child can plot points on a graph in the first quadrant and understand what the points mean in real-life or math problems.

subject: Math
description: Understand that the x-coordinate, the first number in an ordered pair, indicates movement parallel to the x-axis starting at the origin; and the y-coordinate, the second number, indicates movement parallel to the y-axis starting at the origin.
description_plain: Your child knows that the first number in a coordinate pair shows how far to move left or right, and the second number shows how far to move up or down from the starting point.

subject: Math
description: Use a pair of perpendicular number lines (axes) with the intersection of the lines (the origin (0,0)) arranged to coincide with the 0 on each line and a given point in the plane located by using an ordered pair of numbers, called its coordinates.
description_plain: Your child can use two number lines that cross at zero to find and name points using pairs of numbers called coordinates.

subject: Math
description: Describe and understand the key attributes of the coordinate plane.
description_plain: Your child can describe and understand the main parts of the coordinate plane.

subject: Math
description: Graph points on the coordinate plane to solve real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can put points on a graph to help solve real-world and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Apply this technique to solve real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can use measurement techniques to solve real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Find volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a solid made of two separate rectangular boxes by adding their volumes together.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as additive.
description_plain: Recognize volume as additive.

subject: Math
description: Apply the formulas V = l × w × h and V = B × h (where B stands for the area of the base) for rectangular prisms to find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths, and in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole-number sides and solve real-life problems using these formulas.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole-number edge lengths by packing it with unit cubes, and show that the volume is the same as would be found by multiplying the edge lengths, equivalently by multiplying the height by the area of the base.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of a rectangular box by filling it with unit cubes and show that multiplying the length, width, and height gives the same volume.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and addition and solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child understands how volume relates to multiplication and addition and can solve problems about the volume of rectangular boxes.

subject: Math
description: Use concrete and/or visual models to measure the volume of rectangular prisms in cubic units by counting cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and nonstandard units.
description_plain: Your child can measure the volume of rectangular boxes by counting cubic units using models or pictures.

subject: Math
description: A solid figure which can be packed without gaps or overlaps using n unit cubes is said to have a volume of n cubic units.
description_plain: Your child understands that a solid filled exactly with a certain number of unit cubes has a volume equal to that number of cubes.

subject: Math
description: A cube with side length 1 unit, called a "unit cube," is said to have "one cubic unit" of volume, and can be used to measure volume.
description_plain: Your child knows that a cube with sides of one unit has a volume of one cubic unit and can use it to measure volume.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of solid figures and understand volume measurement in terms of cubic units.
description_plain: Your child understands that volume is how much space a solid object takes up and measures it using cubic units.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: Understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child learns what volume means and how it connects to multiplication and addition.

subject: Math
description: Use graphic displays of data (line plots (dot plots), tables, etc.) to solve real-world problems using fractional data.
description_plain: Your child can use graphs and tables with fractional data to solve real-world problems.

subject: Math
description: Interpret numerical data, with whole-number values, represented with tables or line plots.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain data shown in tables or line plots with whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Collect, represent, and interpret numerical data, including whole numbers, and fractional and decimal values.
description_plain: Your child can collect, show, and understand data using whole numbers, fractions, and decimals.

subject: Math
description: Represent and interpret data.
description_plain: Represent and interpret data.

subject: Math
description: Convert among different-sized standard measurement units within a given measurement system. Use conversions in solving multi-step, real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can change measurements from one unit to another within the same system and use these changes to solve multi-step real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Convert like measurement units within a given measurement system.
description_plain: Your child can convert between units that measure the same thing within one measurement system.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving division of unit fractions by nonzero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions by using visual fraction models and/or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems that involve dividing unit fractions by whole numbers and dividing whole numbers by unit fractions using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Represent division of a whole number by a unit fraction, and compute such quotients using a visual fraction model. Use the relationship between multiplication and division to explain that a ÷ 1/b because ab × 1/b = a.
description_plain: Your child can show and calculate dividing a whole number by a unit fraction using pictures and explain why it works using multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Represent division of a unit fraction by a nonzero whole number and compute such quotients using a visual fraction model. Use the relationship between multiplication and division to explain that 1/b ÷ c = 1/bc because 1/bc × c = 1/b.
description_plain: Your child can show and calculate dividing a unit fraction by a whole number using pictures and explain why it works using multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of division to divide unit fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by unit fractions.
description_plain: Your child can divide fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by fractions, and solve real-life problems using these skills.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers by using visual fraction models and/or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems by multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction greater than 1 results in a product greater than the given number, explaining why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than 1 results in a product smaller than the given number, and relating the principle of fraction equivalence a/b = n × a/n × b to the effect of multiplying a/b by 1.
description_plain: Your child can explain why multiplying by a fraction greater than 1 makes a number bigger and why multiplying by a fraction less than 1 makes it smaller, and understand how multiplying by 1 keeps a fraction the same.

subject: Math
description: Comparing the size of a fractional product to the size of one factor on the basis of the size of the other factor, without performing the indicated multiplication.
description_plain: Your child can compare the size of a fraction multiplication result to one of the numbers without doing the full multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication as scaling (resizing), by:
description_plain: Your child understands multiplication as resizing or scaling a number.

subject: Math
description: Multiply fractional side lengths to find areas of rectangles, and represent fraction products as rectangular areas.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles by multiplying fractional side lengths and show the answer as a rectangle.

subject: Math
description: Show that the area is the same by tiling as would be found by multiplying the side lengths.
description_plain: Your child can show that the area found by covering a rectangle with tiles is the same as multiplying its side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Tile it with unit squares of the appropriate unit fraction side lengths.
description_plain: Your child can cover a rectangle with small squares that match fractional side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Find the area of a rectangle with fractional side lengths.
description_plain: Your child can find the area of rectangles that have fractional side lengths.

subject: Math
description: Interpret the product (a/b) × q as a parts of a partitions of q into b equal parts, and equivalently, as the result of the sequence of operations a × q ÷ b.
description_plain: Your child understands multiplying a fraction by a whole number as taking parts of that number or doing multiplication and division steps.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication to multiply a fraction or whole number by a fraction.
description_plain: Your child can multiply fractions and whole numbers by fractions, building on what they already know about multiplication.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers by using visual fraction models and/or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction means dividing the top number by the bottom number and can solve word problems that result in fractions or mixed numbers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child can multiply and divide fractions by building on what they already know about multiplication and division.

subject: Math
description: Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fraction to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can use common fractions and number sense to estimate answers and check if they make sense.

subject: Math
description: Justify the conclusions by using visual fraction models and/or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can explain their answers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole (the whole can be a set of objects), including cases of unlike denominators.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with the same whole, including different denominators, using pictures or equations, and estimate answers using common fractions.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different bottom numbers (including mixed numbers) by changing them to equivalent fractions with the same bottom number.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child can use equivalent fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math
description: Relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
description_plain: Your child can explain their math steps and reasoning when solving problems.

subject: Math
description: Use concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction and between multiplication and division.
description_plain: Your child can use models, drawings, and place value strategies to add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals.

subject: Math
description: Add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to hundredths.
description_plain: Your child can add, subtract, multiply, and divide decimals to two decimal places.

subject: Math
description: Illustrate and explain the calculation by using equations, rectangular arrays, and/or area models.
